creator: chadf@triularity.org
text:
I've been keeping COMPAT_FREEBSD11 in the kernel config as a workaround, and hadn't checked since. I just tried it again with a 14.1 system and the issue still exists.

I recently noticed kern.features.compat_freebsdXX entries in sysctl. I don't know if these were added after I mentioned the general idea, or if I simply missed them the first time. As long as bootstrap-openjdk8 is an older pre-compiled binary, the only viable option appears to be requiring 11.x compatibility enabled.

Perhaps one option would be to add a pre-install check to the bootstrap-openjdk8 ports Makefile (and/or PRE-INSTALL package script) with an assertion like:

   if [ X`sysctl -qn kern.features.compat_freebsd11` != X1 ]; then
        echo "FreeBSD 11.x binary compatibility is require in the kernel for this package" >&2
        exit 1
   fi

At least this way the end user would know why it doesn't work, rather than generically failing after the fact.  If this solution is thought to be a good idea and no one else wants to do it, I could try to submit a patch when I have time.

Another side thought.. since this problem can apply to any pre-compiled port, it maybe long term beneficial to add a common /Mk/*.mk framework for this. Then a Makefile need only list which version(s) of binary support is required, and it handles the rest.
